rule_text: All AP Government stores procurement shall be done THROUGH the Director, Printing, Stationery & Stores Purchase Department (Central Stores Purchase, CSP) which has State-wide jurisdiction — except for items specifically exempted in Article 123 (perishable items, controlled commodities like cement/iron/steel under bulk contract, journals, low-value items <= Rs.2,000, emergency machinery spares, fuel, etc.) OR where the Stores Purchase Committee has granted special exemption.
verification_method: For Goods tenders >= Rs.2,000: check tender was routed through CSP Director OR documented Article-123-exemption category OR Stores Purchase Committee exemption order

rule_text: AP departments may directly procure office-common-use articles only if estimated value does NOT exceed Rs.2,000 AND the item is NOT on a rate or running contract; OR for items whose individual unit value <= Rs.500 with maximum aggregate Rs.5,000 at a time, also subject to no rate/running contract.
verification_method: For direct departmental Goods purchases: aggregate value <= Rs.2,000 OR per-unit <= Rs.500 with cap Rs.5,000; check no rate/running contract exists for the item

rule_text: AP Government Khadi requirements shall be placed on the Andhra Pradesh Khadi & Village Industries Board for supply at the rates approved/certified by the Certification Committee of the Commission; CSP and other departments may tap other sources only after the Board expresses inability to supply.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123(x) — Memo No 64219/2145/Accts/63-2 dated 17-1-1964
verification_method: Khadi tenders must show Board-non-supply certificate before sourcing elsewhere
